purpose.summary: This copybook defines the structure of PADFLPCB, which appears to be a PCB (Program Communication Block) used in IMS (Information Management System) for database communication. It contains fields related to the database name, segment level, PCB status, processing options, segment name, key feedback, number of sensitive segments, and key feedback area.

paragraphs.purpose: This copybook defines the structure of the PADFLPCB, which is likely a Program Communication Block (PCB) used within an IMS (Information Management System) environment for interacting with databases. The PCB provides a communication interface between the application program and the IMS database. It contains various fields that describe the database, segment information, and processing options. PADFL-DBDNAME (line 18) stores the name of the database. PADFL-SEG-LEVEL (line 19) indicates the segment level. PADFL-PCB-STATUS (line 20) provides the status of the PCB. PADFL-PCB-PROCOPT (line 21) specifies the processing options. PADFL-SEG-NAME (line 23) contains the name of the segment. PADFL-KEYFB-NAME (line 24) and PADFL-NUM-SENSEGS (line 25) store key feedback name and number of sensitive segments respectively. PADFL-KEYFB (line 26) is a larger field that holds the key feedback area, providing information about the last accessed segment's key.
paragraphs.dead_code_reason: Record layout 'PADFLPCB' is never used by any program
